gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found that adding vLLM/SGLang Poetry extras made even documented installs unsatisfiable and that the first vLLM smoke tokenizers pin was unpublished. I removed the conflicting engine packages from Poetry metadata, documented separate serving environments, pinned the vLLM smoke to a resolver-compatible published stack, and final re-review returned clean.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_vllm_smoke.py tests/test_project_governance.py tests/test_public_package.py -q
what_changed: Updated the Databricks vLLM smoke helper to install vLLM 0.23.0 with a resolver-compatible pinned transitive stack.
Kept vLLM and SGLang out of Poetry extras because current engine releases require incompatible Torch/Transformers/xgrammar dependency stacks.
Documented that established serving engines are installed in separate serving environments while this package keeps the engine handoff contract and Databricks smoke orchestration.
why: The package must integrate with established serving engines without making the core Poetry project or documented test installs unsatisfiable.
